methodology: Checked comments on winner posts by navigating to post pages and extracting visible commenter names

checked_posts.title: Airwwave (AR music conductor)
checked_posts.creator: Lee Black
checked_posts.note: Lee Black himself replied to most comments — active engagement with community

checked_posts.title: Web Poetry (digital handwriting)
checked_posts.creator: Cara Ellis
checked_posts.note: Pure community engagement — no judges visible

checked_posts.title: Visual Atlas (AI-powered archive explorer)
checked_posts.creator: Bene Brandhofer
checked_posts.note: Lowest engagement winner — won on design quality, not popularity

staff_post_impressions.source: Top 50 posts per edition by engagement (unauthenticated API)

top_impression_post.author: gui_seiz
top_impression_post.note: likely winner announcement — highest reach post in any edition

key_finding: Contra staff did NOT comment on winner submissions. Judging was external — winners were selected based on project quality reviewed off-platform. Staff presence was exclusively via high-reach announcement posts (winner reveals, kickoffs). The biggest audience touchpoint: Gui Seiz's March 2026 post had 91,802 impressions.
implication: You cannot identify likely winners by looking for judge engagement on Contra. The absence of judge comments on a post does not indicate disqualification.
